_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The EPA HD Phase 3 GHG rule created no EPR-style producer registration requirement and no jurisdictional registration obligation for freight forwarders, transport operators, or fleet buyers. All registration and certification obligations under the rule were imposed exclusively on vehicle and engine manufacturers through EPA's certification program (codified in 40 C.F.R. Parts 86, 1036, 1037, 1039, 1054, and 1065). Manufacturers are required to submit certification applications to EPA prior to producing and selling covered vehicles, and to designate covered components (including, for ZEVs, all components needed to charge, store, and transmit power) in those applications.

The Averaging, Banking, and Trading (ABT) program — updated under Phase 3 — requires manufacturers, not fleet operators or forwarders, to track and report credit generation and use across three weight-class averaging sets. No reporting portal, registration window, or compliance filing deadline exists for downstream operators.

**Published requirements:** Manufacturer certification and ABT reporting structures are codified and were published with the final rule on April 22, 2024 (89 FR 29440). The rule correction was published June 17, 2024.

**Status as of February 2026:** The EPA rescission of the endangerment finding (published February 18, 2026) explicitly states that "engine and vehicle manufacturers no longer have any future obligations for the measurement, control, and reporting of GHG emissions for any highway engine and vehicle." This eliminates the certification and ABT reporting obligations at the federal level, pending litigation outcomes.

**Data fields the workspace should collect operationally (not regulatory registration, but compliance intelligence):** For each road transport carrier used on US domestic legs — particularly for time-critical cargo (live events, fine art, humanitarian): (1) vehicle model year and class (Class 2b–8); (2) powertrain type (ICE diesel, CNG/LNG, PHEV, BEV, FCEV); (3) carrier's California ACF/ACT registration status if operating in CARB states; (4) any contractual GHG benchmarks embedded in the workspace's shipper agreements that reference Phase 3 or Scope 3 emissions metrics. These fields are needed both for client reporting and to quickly operationalize compliance if litigation restores Phase 3 obligations.

*Source: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles—Phase 3, 89 F.R. 29440 (April 22, 2024), codified at 40 C.F.R. §§ 86, 1036, 1037, 1039, 1054, 1065. https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3. Final Rule: Rescission of the GHG Endangerment Finding, EPA, February 12, 2026. https://www.epa.gov/regulations-emissions-vehicles-and-engines/final-rule-rescission-greenhouse-gas-endangerment*
